title: ConvertHub - All-in-One File Conversion Toolkit

requirements.text: Create a hero section at the top of the app with the 'ConvertHub' logo/name, a tagline ('All-in-One File Conversion Toolkit' or similar), and a subtle animated logo mark. Use Google Fonts (Inter or Poppins) for all typography.

quotes: Hero section with app name, tagline, and animated logo
Beautiful typography using Google Fonts (Inter or Poppins)

acceptanceCriteria: App name 'ConvertHub' is prominently displayed in the hero section
A descriptive tagline is visible below the app name
Google Fonts (Inter or Poppins) is loaded and applied to all text
Hero section has an animated logo or icon element

requirements.text: Implement a dark glassmorphism visual theme with an animated purple/blue gradient background. The background gradient should slowly and continuously shift colors. All cards and modals should use glass-effect styling (backdrop blur, semi-transparent backgrounds, subtle borders). Apply neon glow effects on active/focused elements. Include floating particles or a subtle ambient background animation.

quotes: Dark glassmorphism theme with purple/blue gradient background
Animated gradient background (slowly shifting colors)
Glass-effect cards with blur and subtle borders
Neon glow effects on active elements
Floating particles or subtle background animation

acceptanceCriteria: Background displays a dark purple/blue gradient that animates smoothly in a continuous loop
Tool cards have frosted-glass appearance (backdrop-filter blur, semi-transparent fill, visible border)
Focused/active UI elements display a neon glow effect
Floating particles or ambient animation is visible in the background without obscuring content
Theme is visually consistent across all sections

requirements.text: Implement a responsive tool grid layout displaying all 8 conversion tools as cards: PDF to Image, Image to PDF, Image to Word, PDF to Word, Word to PDF, Image Compressor, Image Resizer, and Merge PDFs. The grid should be 4 columns on desktop, 2 on tablet, and 1 on mobile. Each card must have a unique colorful icon with a gradient accent, a tool name, and a short description. Cards must have smooth hover animations (scale, glow, lift effect).

quotes: Tool grid (4 columns on desktop, 2 on tablet, 1 on mobile)
Each tool card has a unique colorful icon and gradient accent
Smooth hover animations on tool cards
Responsive grid layout for tool cards

acceptanceCriteria: All 8 tool cards are rendered in the grid
Grid is 4 columns on desktop (≥1024px), 2 on tablet (≥640px), 1 on mobile
Each card has a distinct icon with a gradient color accent unique to that tool
Hovering a card triggers a smooth lift/scale/glow animation
Tool name and description are clearly legible on each card

requirements.text: Each tool card should open its conversion interface in a modal or dedicated expanded section. Each tool interface must include: a drag-and-drop file upload zone with animated dashed/glowing border, a file input fallback button, relevant conversion options (e.g., output format selector for PDF to Image), a 'Convert' action button, a gradient-filled progress bar shown during processing, and a download button that triggers file download after conversion completes. Show toast notifications for success and error states.

quotes: Each tool opens in a modal or dedicated section
Drag & drop file upload zones with animated borders
Progress bars with gradient fills
File download triggers after conversion
Toast notifications for success/error states

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a tool card opens a modal or expands a dedicated section for that tool
Each tool interface has a drag-and-drop upload zone with an animated border effect
A file input button is available as fallback to drag-and-drop
A gradient-filled progress bar is shown during conversion processing
After successful conversion, a download button appears and triggers file download
Toast notifications appear for both success and error outcomes
Modals can be dismissed/closed

requirements.text: Implement the PDF to Image conversion tool (client-side). Use PDF.js from CDN to render each page of an uploaded PDF and export pages as JPG or PNG images. Allow the user to select output format (JPG or PNG). Provide a download for all converted images (individually or as a zip).

quotes: PDF to Image (PDF → JPG/PNG)
Use PDF.js (from CDN) for PDF rendering/reading
All processing done client-side (no server needed)

acceptanceCriteria: User can upload a PDF file
User can select JPG or PNG as output format
Each PDF page is rendered and exported as an image using PDF.js
Converted images are downloadable
Processing is entirely client-side

requirements.text: Implement the Image to PDF conversion tool (client-side). Use jsPDF from CDN to combine one or more uploaded JPG/PNG images into a single PDF document. Allow uploading multiple images and define their order before conversion.

quotes: Image to PDF (JPG/PNG → PDF)
Use jsPDF (from CDN) for creating PDFs
All processing done client-side (no server needed)

acceptanceCriteria: User can upload one or more JPG/PNG files
All uploaded images are combined into a single downloadable PDF
Processing is entirely client-side using jsPDF

requirements.text: Implement the Image to Word (DOCX) conversion tool (client-side). Embed the uploaded image(s) into a DOCX file using a client-side library (e.g., docx.js or similar). Provide the DOCX file for download.

quotes: Image to Word (JPG/PNG → DOCX)
Use Mammoth.js or similar for Word conversion
All processing done client-side (no server needed)

acceptanceCriteria: User can upload a JPG or PNG image
A DOCX file is generated containing the image
The DOCX file is downloadable
Processing is entirely client-side

requirements.text: Implement the PDF to Word (DOCX) conversion tool (client-side). Use PDF.js to extract text content from each page of the uploaded PDF, then generate a DOCX file with the extracted text using a client-side DOCX library. Provide the DOCX for download.

quotes: PDF to Word (PDF → DOCX)
Use PDF.js (from CDN) for PDF rendering/reading
All processing done client-side (no server needed)

acceptanceCriteria: User can upload a PDF file
Text is extracted from the PDF using PDF.js
A DOCX file is generated from the extracted text
The DOCX file is downloadable
Processing is entirely client-side

requirements.text: Implement the Word to PDF conversion tool (client-side). Use Mammoth.js from CDN to read the uploaded DOCX file and extract its HTML content, then use jsPDF to render that content into a downloadable PDF.

quotes: Word to PDF (DOCX → PDF)
Use Mammoth.js or similar for Word conversion
Use jsPDF (from CDN) for creating PDFs

acceptanceCriteria: User can upload a DOCX file
The DOCX content is parsed using Mammoth.js
A PDF is generated from the content using jsPDF
The PDF is downloadable
Processing is entirely client-side

requirements.text: Implement the Image Compressor tool (client-side). Use the Canvas API to re-encode an uploaded JPG or PNG at a user-selected quality level (e.g., a slider from 10–100%). Display the original file size and the compressed file size before download. Provide the compressed image for download.

quotes: Image Compressor (Reduce image file size)
Use Canvas API for image manipulation
All processing done client-side (no server needed)

acceptanceCriteria: User can upload a JPG or PNG image
A quality slider or input allows the user to choose compression level
Original and estimated compressed file sizes are displayed
Compressed image is downloadable
Processing uses the Canvas API client-side

requirements.text: Implement the Image Resizer tool (client-side). Use the Canvas API to resize an uploaded JPG or PNG to user-specified width and height dimensions. Provide an option to maintain aspect ratio. Display a preview of the resized image and allow download.

quotes: Image Resizer (Resize images to custom dimensions)
Use Canvas API for image manipulation
All processing done client-side (no server needed)

acceptanceCriteria: User can upload a JPG or PNG image
Width and height inputs accept custom pixel values
An option to lock/maintain aspect ratio is available
A preview or confirmation of the resized dimensions is shown
Resized image is downloadable
Processing uses the Canvas API client-side

requirements.text: Implement the Merge PDFs tool (client-side). Allow uploading multiple PDF files and reordering them (drag-to-reorder or up/down buttons). Use PDF.js to read pages from each PDF and jsPDF or pdf-lib (from CDN) to combine all pages into one merged PDF document available for download.

quotes: Merge PDFs (Combine multiple PDFs into one)
Use PDF.js (from CDN) for PDF rendering/reading
All processing done client-side (no server needed)

acceptanceCriteria: User can upload two or more PDF files
Uploaded PDFs are listed with the ability to reorder them
All PDFs are merged into a single downloadable PDF
Processing is entirely client-side

requirements.text: Add a footer section below the tool grid with the app name 'ConvertHub', a brief description, and a note that all conversions are processed locally in the browser (privacy-first messaging).

quotes: Footer with app info
All processing done client-side (no server needed)

acceptanceCriteria: Footer is visible at the bottom of the page
Footer displays the app name and a short description
Footer mentions that files are processed locally / never uploaded to a server

requirements.text: Generate a ConvertHub wordmark/logo image and a hero background image for the landing page.

quotes: animated logo
Make it look like a premium SaaS product

acceptanceCriteria: A logo/wordmark image is rendered in the hero section
A decorative hero background or banner image is used in the hero area

constraints: All file processing (conversion, compression, resizing) must be entirely client-side — no files are uploaded to any server or backend
Use CDN-hosted libraries for PDF.js, jsPDF, Mammoth.js, and any DOCX generation library
The backend (Motoko) should remain minimal — no file processing logic in the backend
Use purple/blue color palette as specified by the user for the glassmorphism theme
nonGoals: Storing or persisting uploaded files or conversion history
Batch conversion queuing beyond what's needed for Merge PDFs
Cloud storage integrations (Google Drive, Dropbox, etc.)

required: ConvertHub abstract geometric logo mark — interlocking arrows or conversion symbol in vibrant purple-to-cyan gradient on dark background (converthub-logo.dim_256x256.png)
Wide hero banner with dark deep-space background featuring soft glowing purple and blue nebula-like gradients, subtle geometric grid lines, and floating abstract shapes (hero-bg.dim_1920x600.png)
